title: React: Component-Based Browser Applications
summary: A React 19.2 course covering components, JSX, props, state, events, forms, hooks, effects, data loading, routing, context, accessibility, performance and user-centered tests.

assumed_knowledge: JavaScript through modules, DOM, promises, async/await and fetch
audience.target_capability: Independently design, implement, test and debug an accessible single-page React application with explicit state ownership, routed views and robust asynchronous states.

includes: Fetch-backed loading, empty, success and failure states
React Testing Library and an intermediate SPA
non_goals: Redux or another external state library
Legacy class components as an implementation requirement

runtime.execution_environment: Sandboxed browser origin with mocked APIs and pinned exact dependency lockfile
notes: Exact patch versions are pinned by each published course release.
Server Components and framework-specific server behavior are excluded from Launch 1.

modules.title: React model and project setup
modules.description: Create a client application and understand render, commit and browser ownership boundaries.

skills.title: Application root and rendering
skills.description: Mount one React root and distinguish rendering descriptions from DOM commit work.
outcomes: Create and explain a minimal React root and component tree.

skills.title: Vite project and dependency pinning
skills.description: Run development, build and preview commands while preserving a reviewed lockfile.
outcomes: Create and reproduce a React project from its manifest and lockfile.

skills.title: Declarative UI and re-rendering
skills.description: Describe UI as a function of current props and state rather than imperative DOM mutations.
outcomes: Translate a manual DOM update into state-driven rendering and trace its rerender.

skills.title: React developer tools
skills.description: Inspect component trees, props, state and render causes without editing production state as a fix.
outcomes: Locate and explain a component data-flow defect using developer-tool evidence.

modules.title: Components and JSX
modules.description: Create pure reusable components and valid semantic element trees.

skills.title: Function components
skills.description: Define capitalized pure component functions with a single responsibility.
outcomes: Extract and test a cohesive component without hidden mutation.

skills.title: JSX expressions and attributes
skills.description: Embed expressions, use React DOM property names and keep data distinct from executable markup.
outcomes: Write and debug JSX with dynamic content, attributes and safe text interpolation.

skills.title: Conditional rendering
skills.description: Render explicit loading, empty, success and conditional branches without leaking falsey values accidentally.
outcomes: Implement and test complete conditional UI states.

skills.title: Lists and stable keys
skills.description: Render collections with stable identity keys rather than positions that change with data.
outcomes: Render and update a reorderable list while explaining key-based reconciliation.

modules.title: Props and composition
modules.description: Design one-way component interfaces and compose reusable page structures.

skills.title: Props and one-way data flow
skills.description: Treat props as read-only inputs and send changes upward through callbacks.
outcomes: Trace data and event flow across a parent-child component pair.

skills.title: Children and composition
skills.description: Compose layout and behavior through children or focused slots rather than prop explosions.
outcomes: Design and implement a reusable composition boundary for varied child content.

skills.title: Component API design
skills.description: Keep component props minimal, cohesive, serializable where practical and semantically named.
outcomes: Review and simplify a component interface while documenting its contract.

skills.title: Render purity and Strict Mode
skills.description: Avoid side effects during render and use development double invocation to expose impurities.
outcomes: Find and remove a render-time mutation revealed by Strict Mode.

modules.title: State and events
modules.description: Model interactive state minimally and update it through accessible event handlers.

skills.title: useState and snapshots
skills.description: Treat render state as a snapshot and use updater functions for queued changes based on prior state.
outcomes: Trace and implement state updates without stale arithmetic or direct mutation.

skills.title: Event handlers
skills.description: Pass handlers rather than calling them during render and preserve native element semantics.
outcomes: Implement keyboard- and pointer-compatible interactions with correct handler arguments.

skills.title: Minimal and normalized state
skills.description: Avoid redundant, contradictory or duplicated state and derive values during render when cheap.
outcomes: Refactor a component to one source of truth and explain each remaining state variable.

skills.title: Lifting and preserving state
skills.description: Place shared state at the nearest common owner and use identity or keys to preserve or reset it deliberately.
outcomes: Repair unsynchronized sibling state and control when form state resets.

modules.title: Forms and complex state
modules.description: Build accessible controlled forms and use reducers for related state transitions.

skills.title: Controlled inputs
skills.description: Bind value or checked state to React while preserving labels, input semantics and responsive updates.
outcomes: Implement and test text, choice and checkbox controls without controlled/uncontrolled warnings.

skills.title: Validation and error recovery
skills.description: Combine native constraints and domain validation with persistent instructions and focus-aware errors.
outcomes: Implement and test a form's invalid, submitting, success and server-error states.

skills.title: useReducer
skills.description: Represent related state transitions as explicit actions and a pure reducer.
outcomes: Refactor complex interdependent state into a tested reducer.

skills.title: Reducer and context boundary
skills.description: Share a reducer-backed feature state through a focused provider without making all state global.
outcomes: Design and test a provider boundary for a multi-component feature.

modules.title: Effects, refs and external systems
modules.description: Synchronize with non-React systems while keeping derived logic out of effects.

skills.title: When an effect is needed
skills.description: Use effects only to synchronize with external systems, not for event logic or derivable values.
outcomes: Remove an unnecessary effect and place logic in render or an event handler.

skills.title: Dependencies and reactive values
skills.description: Declare every reactive dependency and restructure code instead of suppressing the hooks linter.
outcomes: Repair stale-closure and runaway-effect defects with correct dependencies.

skills.title: Cleanup and race prevention
skills.description: Undo subscriptions and ignore or abort obsolete asynchronous results across rerenders and unmounts.
outcomes: Implement and test setup-cleanup symmetry and latest-request behavior.

skills.title: Refs and imperative escape hatches
skills.description: Store non-rendering values and access DOM nodes only for focus, measurement or external integration.
outcomes: Implement an accessible focus transition without using refs as hidden render state.

modules.title: Remote data and asynchronous UI
modules.description: Load and mutate remote data with complete observable states and stale-work protection.

skills.title: Loading, empty, success and failure states
skills.description: Model remote data as explicit states rather than ambiguous null checks.
outcomes: Render and test every observable remote-data state.

skills.title: Client fetch lifecycle
skills.description: Trigger bounded requests, validate responses and prevent obsolete results from winning.
outcomes: Implement a fetch-backed component with cancellation and retry behavior.

skills.title: Mutations and pending UI
skills.description: Coordinate form mutation, pending feedback, success reconciliation and recoverable failure without duplicate submission.
outcomes: Implement and test a remote mutation with disabled-state and error-recovery semantics.

skills.title: Custom hooks for reusable logic
skills.description: Extract reusable stateful behavior without hiding dependencies or returning unstable accidental APIs.
outcomes: Design and test a focused custom hook used by two components.

modules.title: Routing and application context
modules.description: Map URL state to views and share truly global services through narrow providers.

skills.title: Declarative routes and links
skills.description: Map paths to page-level components and navigate with real links that preserve browser behavior.
outcomes: Implement nested routes and verify direct, link and back-forward navigation.

skills.title: Parameters, search state and not-found views
skills.description: Parse route and query values as untrusted input and render explicit missing-resource states.
outcomes: Implement and test parameterized and filterable views with invalid and unknown URLs.

skills.title: Route titles and focus management
skills.description: Update document context and move focus appropriately after client navigation.
outcomes: Verify route changes are perceivable to keyboard and assistive-technology users.

skills.title: Context for stable global dependencies
skills.description: Use context for theme, session or service boundaries without placing rapidly changing local state globally.
outcomes: Design and test a narrow context provider with a documented default and consumer contract.

modules.title: Accessibility, errors and performance
modules.description: Keep component applications resilient, perceivable and appropriately optimized.

skills.title: Semantic and keyboard-accessible components
skills.description: Render native elements, visible focus and logical order instead of div-based imitation controls.
outcomes: Audit and repair component names, roles, states, focus order and keyboard behavior.

skills.title: Error boundaries and recovery
skills.description: Contain render failures at meaningful regions and provide recovery without masking event or async errors.
outcomes: Design and test a route or feature error boundary with useful fallback and reset behavior.

skills.title: Measure before optimization
skills.description: Use React performance tools to identify actual expensive renders before memoizing.
outcomes: Record a render-performance observation and justify either a targeted optimization or no change.

skills.title: Memoization and stable identity
skills.description: Use memo, useMemo or useCallback only when measured work or dependency identity requires it.
outcomes: Repair premature or ineffective memoization and demonstrate any retained optimization with evidence.

modules.title: User-centered tests and SPA project
modules.description: Verify behavior through accessible queries and deliver an intermediate routed application.

skills.title: Accessible queries and interactions
skills.description: Query rendered output by role, name, label or text and drive realistic user interactions.
outcomes: Write component tests that avoid implementation-detail selectors.

skills.title: Asynchronous component tests
skills.description: Wait for observable state transitions and mock the network boundary rather than internal hooks.
outcomes: Test loading, success, empty, error, retry and stale-request behavior deterministically.

skills.title: Routing and integration tests
skills.description: Verify navigation, URL state, direct entry and not-found behavior using a representative router setup.
outcomes: Create an integration test covering route navigation and focus-context changes.

skills.title: Intermediate single-page application
skills.description: Integrate routed views, forms, remote data, complete async states, accessibility, errors and tests.
outcomes: Deliver and explain a reproducible React SPA whose key user journeys pass automated and manual keyboard tests.

exit_outcomes: Design component and prop boundaries with pure rendering, stable identity and one-way data flow.
Model local, shared, reducer and remote state without redundant values or unnecessary effects.
Implement accessible forms, routed views and complete asynchronous loading, success, empty, failure and cancellation states.
Diagnose component behavior with React tools and verify user journeys through accessible queries and realistic interactions.
Deliver a reproducible intermediate React SPA without relying on framework-specific server features or external state libraries.
